source_text: For text, use 10 to 12 point font size. Times New Roman 12-point is the preferred font style and size for official correspondence, but Courier New may be used for informal correspondence.
plain_language_rule: Standard letter body text must use 10 to 12 point font size. Times New Roman 12-point is the preferred font for official correspondence. Courier New may be used for informal correspondence.
renderer_impact: Font selection and sizing throughout document body text; ReportLab font configuration must support Times New Roman 12pt as default, with Courier New as alternative for informal correspondence
notes: IMPORTANT AMBIGUITY: Section 7-2.1 (C7-002) prohibits 'proportional spacing' but Times New Roman (the preferred font per 7-2.2) IS a proportional font. This creates a direct conflict between sections. The prohibition on proportional spacing in 7-2.1 likely refers to character spacing/kerning settings rather than font type, since the manual explicitly approves Times New Roman which is inherently proportional. Clarification needed: 7-2.1's 'proportional spacing' prohibition should be interpreted as forbidding variable letter-spacing adjustments, not proportional font families. Times New Roman 12pt remains the standard for official correspondence despite this apparent conflict. Font size range of 10-12pt provides flexibility; 12pt is preferred for body text. Header elements may use different sizes per their specific requirements.
